# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our application that needs to write logs and temporary files. Copy application files and set up directories for logs and cache. The container needs write permissions to these directories. Keep permissions flexible for ease of development and debugging.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image 
FROM python:3-slim   # Adjust version according your application's requirement, e.g., 'python:3.8', etc...   

ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED True     # Enable buffering in the console output for readability and ease of debugging (optional) 

# Set work directory as /app  
WORKDIR /app      

# Copy requirements file to working dir     
COPY requirement.txt .   

# Install Python dependencies using pip tool, install inside a layer so it's cached by Docker    
RUN apt-get update && \  # Update package lists for new versions of the OS packages used in our image  
apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl python3.8-dev libpq-dev postgresql postgresql-client nginx nodejs wget zip unzip git make gcc && \  # Install dependencies from requirements file, no cache so it's always updated
apt-get clean all   &> /dev/null; rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*     # Remove unused package lists and caches   
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir setuptools wheel && \  # Install dependencies from requirements file, no cache so it's always updated     
pip3 install pytz==2019.3 psycopg2 pgloader pyyaml requests[security] certifi idna beautifulsoup4 lxml Pillow pandas tlder werkzeug cryptography && \  # Install Python dependencies   
apt-get clean all   &> /dev/null; rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*     # Remove unused package lists and caches     
RUN mkdir logs cache       # Create directories for logging purposes         
COPY . ./        # Copy application files to the working directory 
WORKDIR $PWD    # Set work dir back as current location
